title: DiscoveryIntent
description: Structured search contract produced by the Intent Translator from a candidate profile and user instruction. Consumed by career-search-agent as the authoritative guide for query budget allocation, lane prioritisation, and constraint enforcement. Produced deterministically — the translator does not search the web or invent jobs.

intent_kind.description: directed_discovery: user gave concrete targets. profile_based_exploration: user unsure, lanes inferred from profile. gap_fill_discovery: no strong user direction, lanes target weak/missing coverage areas.

raw_user_instruction.description: Original user instruction, preserved verbatim for audit.

profile_id.description: candidate_profile_id of the profile used as input. Stored for traceability.

global_constraints.description: Constraints that apply to all search lanes. Hard constraints override agent strategy; soft preferences guide budget allocation.

hard_constraints.description: Non-negotiable constraints that the agent must not override. Each entry carries a provenance tag so downstream components can distinguish constraints the user stated explicitly from those inferred from the profile or system strategy. source values: 'user_explicit' — stated in the user instruction or search_params; 'profile_derived' — inferred from the candidate profile (only valid when search_source allows profile); 'system_strategy' — derived from strategy_context (never promoted to a hard user constraint). Examples: {"value": "max_years_experience: 3", "source": "user_explicit"}, {"value": "remote_only", "source": "user_explicit"}.

value.description: The constraint as a short human-readable string, e.g. 'max_years_experience: 3', 'location: NYC only', 'exclude: model_validation'.

source.description: Where this constraint originated. user_explicit: user said it directly. profile_derived: inferred from candidate profile. system_strategy: from strategy_context (must NOT be used as a hard user constraint).

soft_preferences.description: Preferences that should guide lane priority and query selection but may be relaxed if a lane is consistently low-yield. Examples: 'prefer mid-size firms', 'prefer direct-hire over contract'.

negative_preferences.description: Directions to deprioritise. Not absolute exclusions unless listed in hard_constraints. Examples: 'do not prioritise pure model validation', 'avoid roles requiring 5+ years'.

search_lanes.description: Ordered list of search lanes. directed_discovery typically has 1-2; profile_based_exploration has 3-5; gap_fill_discovery has 2-4. Each lane is a testable hypothesis with its own query budget.

lane_id.description: Short identifier for the lane, used in candidate logging and coverage report. E.g. 'exposure_management', 'portfolio_analytics'.

workstream_id.description: Primary workstream from workstream_taxonomy.yaml this lane maps to. Optional for multi-workstream lanes.

hypothesis.description: Human-readable reason why this lane is worth searching. Must cite evidence from profile, user signal, or strategy gap — not just job popularity.

evidence_from_profile.description: Specific profile signals that justify this lane. E.g. ['VaR / stress testing experience', 'risk reporting workflow ownership'].

user_signal.description: Fragment of the user instruction that maps to this lane, if any. Empty string if the lane is entirely profile-inferred.

strategy_signal.description: Coverage gap or recommended search from strategy_context that this lane addresses, if any.

query_seeds.description: Concrete job-market search phrases for the discovery agent to use or expand. Must be general job-search language — no personal names, employer-sensitive detail, or fabricated company names.

target_company_types.description: Company type filters for this lane. E.g. ['bank', 'asset_manager', 'insurance_carrier', 'broker_dealer'].

exclude_role_keywords.description: Keywords that indicate the query has drifted out of scope. Agent should avoid these in query expansions for this lane.

budget_share.description: Fraction of total query budget to allocate to this lane. All lanes must sum to 1.0 (enforced by the translator post-processor).

risk_of_false_positive.description: Description of the most likely drift or mismatch risk for this lane. E.g. 'May surface data analyst / BI roles without risk content'.

success_criteria.description: What constitutes a good result for this lane. E.g. '3+ postings with explicit VaR / exposure language at banks or broker-dealers'.

inherited_hard_constraints.description: Copy of global_constraints.hard_constraints propagated to this lane. Populated by the translator post-processor, not the LLM.

source_strategy.description: Source-level guidance derived from strategy_context. Does not override user targets.

prefer_sources.description: Source domains or ATS types confirmed effective in prior runs.

avoid_sources.description: Source domains or ATS types with known access failures (403, bot-block, stale boards).

translator_notes.description: Audit fields written by the translator to explain non-obvious decisions.

assumptions.description: Ambiguous inputs that were interpreted rather than resolved by the user. Record here instead of silently strengthening constraints.

missing_information.description: Profile gaps or instruction ambiguities that limited lane quality. Useful for the UI to prompt the user for more detail.

translator_version.description: Version tag for the translator prompt/logic. Used to invalidate cached intents when the translator is updated.
